title: Relations, Rows, Domains, Keys, and Missing Information · SQL Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Support tickets, teams, members, and events must become a precise relational model without duplicate identity or invented missing values. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a key-and-null-aware relational model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain relations, tuples, attributes, domains, degree, cardinality, and why row order is not relational meaning.
Distinguish superkeys, candidate keys, primary keys, alternate keys, and stable surrogate identifiers.
Model optional and required references with foreign keys and predict update or delete consequences.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Relation Domain
steps.instruction:
Order the Relation Domain evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Relation Domain.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Relation Domain assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Relation Domain, keep Relation Domain connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: Model teams, members, tickets, and events where members may be unassigned, ticket titles repeat, and one ticket has 2 events. Declare row grain, candidate keys, cardinalities, NULL meaning, dependencies, and lossless relationships.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Relation Domain has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Relation Domain, name the row grain, Relation Domain precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Declare row grain, candidate keys, cardinalities, NULL meaning, dependencies, and lossless relationships.
For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Relation Domain, challenge the documented misconception with result, constraint, or plan evidence: A table is defined by its visible row order.

options.text: Declare stakeholder question, input relations, row grain, keys, NULL meaning, and expected cardinality for the quiz Relation Domain case
Predict relational transformations, constraint or transaction effects, and dialect boundary before execution for the quiz Relation Domain case
Run the smallest browser SQL statement and retain result rows, changed count, error, or query plan for the quiz Relation Domain case
Reconcile totals and invariants, test changed data, and document security, recovery, and portability limits for the quiz Relation Domain case

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
